text:
'Mann Ki Baat': PM Modi praises Project Pari for beautifying public spaces

In the 112th episode of 'Mann Ki Baat', Prime Minister Narendra Modi said, "A country can progress only by taking pride in its culture. Many such efforts are being made in India, one of which is Project Pari. You might have noticed the beautiful paintings on roadside walls and in underpasses. These artworks are created by artists associated with Project Pari."

Mann ki Baat Live: Khadi sales surge by 400%, PM says

The turnover of Khadi Village Industries has surpassed Rs 1.5 lakh crore for the first time, with Khadi sales increasing by 400%. This surge in sales is also generating numerous new employment opportunities. As most of the workers in this industry are women, they are the ones reaping the maximum benefits.

PM Modi introduces 'Manas', new helpline for drug rehabilitation

The government has opened a special center named 'Manas', making it a major step in the fight against drugs. Recently, the helpline and portal for 'Manas' were launched. The government has also issued a toll-free number, '1933'. Anyone can call this number to receive necessary advice or information related to rehabilitation, says PM Modi.

Mann ki Baat Live: PM Modi on Unnati self help group

"Let's talk about colours—colours that have brought prosperity to the lives of over 250 women in Rohtak district, Haryana. These women, who are part of the handloom industry, previously managed small shops and did minor work to make ends meet. However, driven by the desire to progress, they decided to join the Unnati Self-Help Group. Through this group, they received training in block printing and dyeing, transforming their lives," said PM Modi

PM Modi invites tourists to India's newest world heritage site

In the 112th episode of 'Mann Ki Baat', Prime Minister Narendra Modi said, "With Charaideo Maidam now a World Heritage Site, it will attract more tourism. I encourage you to include this site in your future travel plans. On March 9 of this year, I had the honor of unveiling the tallest statue of the great Ahom warrior Lachit Borphukan."

Project Pari: PM Modi urges greater focus on public art in India

A country can progress only by taking pride in its culture. Many such efforts are being made in India, one of which is Project Pari, meaning Public Art of India. For example, at Bharat Mandapam in Delhi, you can see incredible artworks from across the nation. I urge art and culture enthusiasts to contribute more to public art initiatives.

Mann ki Baat Live: PM Modi celebrates India's top 5 finish at International Mathematics Olympiad

In the 112th episode of 'Mann Ki Baat', Prime Minister Narendra Modi said, "Recently, the world witnessed the Olympics of mathematics - the International Mathematics Olympiad. Youth from over 100 countries participated, and our team achieved remarkable success, securing a spot in the top five overall."

'Cheer for Bharat': PM Modi urges nation to support and encourage Paris Olympics athletes

In the 112th episode of 'Mann Ki Baat', Prime Minister Narendra Modi says "In the entire world, Paris Olympic is all over there at this point of time. Our players have the opportunity to let our tricolour at the world level, they have this opportunity to do something for the country. You all should encourage them."

Mann ki Baat Live: PM on Assam's Charaideo Maidams

In the 112th episode of 'Mann Ki Baat', Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced, "Charaideo Maidam of Assam is being included in the Unesco World Heritage Sites. This will be India's 43rd site on the list and the first from the Northeast. Charaideo, meaning 'a shining city on the hills,' was the first capital of the Ahom dynasty. Traditionally, the people of the Ahom dynasty preserved the mortal remains of their ancestors and their valuables in the Moidams."

PM Modi Mann ki Baat Live: 'Cheer for Bharat'

"At this moment, the Paris Olympics is capturing global attention. The Olympics offer our athletes a chance to proudly wave the tricolor on the world stage and achieve something remarkable for our country. I urge you to support our athletes and Cheer for Bharat!" he said.

Mann ki Baat Live: Prime Minister Narendra Modi interacts with International Mathematics Olympiad champs

A six-member team of Indian students represented India at the 2024 International Mathematics Olympiad, held in Bath, United Kingdom, from July 11 to July 22. The team achieved remarkable success, winning four gold medals and one silver. Prime Minister Narendra Modi congratulated the team, calling the achievement "a matter of immense joy and pride."

PM Modi on International Mathematics Olympiad

Our team performed exceptionally well at the International Mathematics Olympiad, securing four gold medals. Competing against participants from over 100 countries, our team consistently ranks in the top 5.

PM Modi Mann ki Baat Live: BJP chief JP Nadda joins party workers at headquarters for 'Mann ki Baat' episode



'Monthly program on All India Radio connects public with PM's vision'

TS BJP General Secretary Dugyala Pradeep Kumar highlighted that the program, aired on All India Radio, DD National, and DD News on the last Sunday of each month, acts as a vital platform for public engagement. It tackles various public issues and aspirations, conveying the Prime Minister's ideas and messages, and frequently showcases success stories from across the nation.

PM Modi Mann ki Baat Live: BJP sets up LED screens, TVs for 'Mann ki Baat' today

The BJP has instructed party activists and leaders to install large LED screens and TV sets at prominent public locations, particularly community halls, near almost all polling booths. This initiative aims to facilitate public viewing of Prime Minister Narendra Modi's 'Mann Ki Baat' program at 11 am on Sunday.

Mann ki Baat Live: PM Modi engages youth contributions 'Mann ki Baat' episode



Mann ki Baat Live: PM Modi to address 'Mann ki Baat' during minister's council meeting

The ongoing two-day Minister's Council meeting will conclude today, with the Chief Ministers and Deputy Chief Ministers of all states in attendance. During the meeting, the Prime Minister's speech will take place, and the leaders will listen to the 'Mann Ki Baat' program at the BJP headquarters. A photo session with Prime Minister Narendra Modi and all the leaders will also be held.

PM Modi Mann ki Baat Live: 112th episode of 'Mann ki Baat'

Prime Minister Narendra Modi will address the 112th episode of his monthly radio program, 'Mann ki Baat,' on Sunday morning. This marks the second episode of the program since he began his third consecutive term as PM.
title: PM Modi Mann Ki Baat Live: PM Modi urges nation to support Paris Olympics athletes in Mann ki Baat address  - The Times of India
